notes: Rules below represent approved boundary exceptions. Anything not matching a boundary rule is treated as core scope and therefore violation if it uses forbidden terms.

rules.notes: Process execution and runtime control; doubles isolate unsafe process spawning
CLI commands often test with mock filesystem/network; core logic extracted to tested packages
Test harness utilities by definition test with mock environments
E2E integration tests may use controlled doubles for external dependencies
OS-specific PTY handling requires platform-specific test doubles
Filesystem watching interacts with OS; doubles isolate filesystem side effects
Desktop notifications are external system; doubles prevent notification spam in tests
Synchronization with external services; doubles isolate network operations
Log output capture for testing requires mock destinations
External command wrapping; doubles isolate subprocess spawning
Warning system tests use mock collectors for verification
Profile management interacts with filesystem and external processes
Bundle operations interact with filesystem; doubles isolate file I/O
Passthrough mode wraps external processes; doubles isolate execution
Cross-profile orchestration tests may use mock external dependencies
Identity benchmarking uses synthetic test data
Auth file operations interact with filesystem; E2E tests use controlled environments
Auth pool monitoring tests use mock pools for isolation
Prediction engine tests use synthetic training data
